tweets.name: Paul Graham
tweets.text:
Someone asked what advice founders ignore. That they:

1. Should change their name.

2. Should launch fast.

3. Shouldn't treat fundraising as success.

4. Shouldn't assume they can raise because it's time to.

5. Should fire bad people quickly.

6. Shouldn't talk to acquirers.

GStack is great for solo devs and for people making projects from scratch. This much is true. 

But what I learned tonight is that the skills transfer to giant codebases with a lot of tech debt, and I hadn't realized that could work until now. 

Try it!

https://t.co/VPvWDzV5c0

Just created my first PR into the Y Combinator codebase (1.84M lines of code total, this PR was 2400 lines) 

I got the YC internal/bookface codebase to run natively in @conductor_build - and it was hairy since it's a gargantuan codebase that I hadn't worked with in more than a https://t.co/ByXYXFSHJD

tweets.name: Garry Tan
tweets.text:
Some serious reduction in context in this PR 

T1 skills: ~40% reduction (~105 lines / ~1200 tokens saved). These skip AskUserQuestion format, Completeness Principle, Repo Mode, and Search Before Building — none of which they use.

T2 skills: ~15% reduction (~30 lines / ~400 https://t.co/QPKXevKKe2

tweets.name: Reid Hoffman
tweets.text:
@ivanhzhao has a great metaphor on how AI can fix this:  “Language models are the steel beams of organizations.” 

Before steel beams, buildings topped out around five or six floors. Steel didn’t just make buildings taller, but enabled an entirely new structure: the skyscraper.

tweets.name: Reid Hoffman
tweets.text:
Not enough companies are using AI to dissolve the coordination tax.

As you add people and increase scope, the tax on aligning them grows superlinearly.

AI can increase throughput without adding layers of humans whose core job is alignment work.

tweets.name: Balaji Srinivasan
tweets.text:
Libertarianism in theory requires Lee Kuan Yew in practice.

Order and borders are prerequisites for liberty and prosperity.

Tolerance and internationalism enables trade and capitalism.

Pragmatism about the scope of the state minimizes the scope of the state.
